$comment: Pre-existing schema/ensure-script drift baseline for Task #234.
This allowlist is intentionally EMPTY. Every entry that used to live
here was replaced by a real server/migrations/ensure-*.ts script under
Task #235 — see the boot-migrate block in server/index.ts and the
ensure-rbac-tables.ts / ensure-user-token-and-role-cols.ts /
ensure-candidate-classification-and-docs.ts / ensure-candidate-activation-tokens.ts /
ensure-sms-outbox-table.ts / ensure-otp-purpose.ts /
ensure-login-rate-limit-buckets.ts files.
DO NOT add new entries here when introducing new schema. The whole point
of the schema-migration coverage check is to force you to add a real
server/migrations/ensure-*.ts file instead of suppressing the warning.
Format (kept here as documentation for the check parser):
newColumns: { '<table_name>': ['<col1>', '<col2>'] }
newTables:  ['<table_name>', ...]
nullDefaultDrift: { '<table_name>': ['<col1>', '<col2>'] }
nullDefaultDrift (Task #242) suppresses the NOT NULL / DEFAULT mismatch
check for a single column. Use this only when the drift between
shared/schema.ts and the ensure-*.ts script is intentional and
documented inside the ensure-script (e.g. users.role_id is added as
nullable on first boot so seedRbac can backfill before the schema's
.notNull() is enforced). Stale entries fail the build.
